text: Education is one of the main components in order to maintain and develop the nation. In recent years, there has been an increase in juvenile crime committed by students. Many students have engaged in physical fighting, drug abuse, and other acts of deviant behavior. For this reason, it is necessary for schools to develop a curriculum that promotes the student’s character and personality based on religious values. This study used the refraction method (habituation and modeling) in an attempt to develop the students’ character. It is believed that if the school and the surrounding community that support the Islamic education of students, then the students’ character will be developed in line with the desired Islamic character. This study aims to examine the refraction method in developing students’ character in SDN Babakan Sirna Sukabumi. The study covered four aspects of the implementation of refraction in developing students’ character, namely: 1) The purpose of developing students’ character by using refraction method; 2) The forms and practices of refraction used in schools; 3) The assessment of refraction in developing students’ character and the character of students as a result of that; and 4) The factors that support and hamper the development of Islamic character.
explanation: The passage reads like a plausible academic abstract with concrete locality and study aims, suggesting human authorship. Occasional unusual wording ('refraction method') could be a translation artifact or AI generation.
